title: Chewy Caramelized Filipino Chicken Breasts Halo Halo
description: A festive Filipino dish featuring chicken breasts, ferment-kimchi, and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients, served in a ti-leaf wrap, with a nutty and chewy texture, inspired by the traditional halo-halo dessert
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut sugar, fish sauce, lime juice, garlic, and ginger to make the caramel sauce.
Add the chicken breasts to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and bake in the oven for 25 minutes or until cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped nuts to the skillet and cook until fragrant and lightly toasted.
Add the ferment-kimchi to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
To assemble the halo-halo, slice the cooked chicken into thin strips and place on a ti leaf.
Top the chicken with the nut and kimchi mixture, and drizzle with the caramel sauce.
